doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_mpz_init (mpfr/src/pool.c L36-L53). In C, the function either reclaims an mpz_t from the static mpz_tab pool (if n_alloc > 0) or calls the real mpz_init to allocate fresh limb storage; in both cases the SIZ (number of significant limbs) is set to 0 so the value is +0.

The TS port is a vacuous factory returning 0n -- the analogue of a freshly-initialised mpz_t whose value is 0. Per ADR 0003 (bigint-as-mpz), there is no allocator, no pool, no explicit init: a bigint is a JS-runtime managed primitive that exists from the moment of assignment. The function is preserved at the public surface because (a) the C side has the symbol, (b) downstream MPFR callers may transitively reference it via the bigint-as-mpz wrapper layer in future ports, and (c) the round-trip count is part of the harness's vacuity verification.

Returning 0n matches the conventional shape of an init-without-value primitive: in MPFR, mpfr_init2 likewise produces a NaN-or-zero placeholder; in the bigint world, an uninitialised integer is simply 0n.

Tag minimums per Rule 7 inapplicable; no-arg accessor pattern (see mpfr_free_pool, mpfr_mpz_clear precedents).
divergence_from_c:
Four divergences:

  1. Return: C void (mutates z in place) -> TS returns 0n (the value of a freshly-initialised bigint mpz analogue).

  2. Parameter: C mpz_ptr z (out-parameter) -> TS no parameters. The bigint return value IS the initialised mpz; the caller binds it to a name.

  3. Pool: C reclaims from a static thread-local pool when available; TS has no pool (the JS GC handles bigint lifetimes). The functional output -- a +0 mpz -- is identical.

  4. Idempotence: C calling mpfr_mpz_init on a mpz that was previously cleared is the intended pool-reclaim path; in TS each call is independent and always returns 0n.

No invariants beyond the trivial 'returns 0n always'.
refs: mpfr/src/pool.c L36-L53 -- the C reference body.
eval/functions/mpfr_free_pool/spec.json -- precedent (no-arg vacuous accessor returning boolean true).
docs/adr/0003-mpz-api.md -- bigint-as-mpz_t; no pool, no explicit init.
HANDOFF.md gotcha #9 (worklog 020) -- count-passthrough / scalar-marker for void-returning ops.
